extensions.name: ai-agent-suggestion
extensions.summary: Marks tasks proposed by a community AI agent that still require human/operator review before resident contact.
extensions.promotion_notes: Keep as an extension until more AI-agent proposal patterns are represented.

extensions.name: elder-friendly
extensions.summary: Marks scenarios that need slower pacing, lower noise, helper fallback, and resident notice sensitivity.
extensions.promotion_notes: Potential future core candidate if elder-friendly participation becomes a repeated scenario family.

extensions.name: incident-review
extensions.summary: Marks scenarios where incident, obstruction, or notice-failure review may route task outcomes back to an operator.
extensions.promotion_notes: Keep as extension until incident evidence semantics are stable across more task types.

extensions.name: light-social
extensions.summary: Marks low-pressure participation scenarios where residents can join without heavy social networking or public profiles.
extensions.promotion_notes: Potential core candidate if low-pressure social mode becomes central to many resident tasks.

extensions.name: public-notice
extensions.summary: Marks scenarios that need resident-facing notice before scheduling, approval, public posting, notice update, or human helper coordination.
extensions.promotion_notes: Likely core candidate if notice semantics stabilize across AI-proposed tasks, helper coordination, resident-facing property operations, notice updates, and notice-failure incident review. See docs/cacp-public-notice-semantics-draft.md.

extensions.name: robot-assist
extensions.summary: Marks future robot-assisted tasks that require operator approval, manual stop, and privacy boundaries.
extensions.promotion_notes: Keep as extension until robot handoff semantics are validated beyond one patrol scenario.
